mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing sector that reduces construction activity and household wealth, transmitting negative demand shocks to equity markets over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or positive returns dominate when both series fall concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive power vanishes when filtered for non-recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports wage-driven consumption, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS growth show negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering expectations of aggressive rate hikes that compress equity multiples and drive negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears in a multivariate regression controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination of slowing economic activity and restrictive financing conditions suppresses corporate earnings, generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS level fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or positive returns dominate under these conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of industrial production contraction signal a broad-based manufacturing recession that reduces capital expenditure and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The 3-month INDPRO decline fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate above 55%, or the signal is subsumed by broader recession indicators in out-of-sample tests.
